name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Columbia, Sc
description_short: NACA is a nonprofit housing advocacy organization offering character-based mortgage lending and HUD-approved housing counseling to underserved communities nationwide.
description_long:
The Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America (NACA) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union and has spent 36 years as a housing advocacy and lending organization. Originally established to create the first-ever Housing Trust Fund, NACA has evolved into a comprehensive nonprofit serving over 3 million people with a focus on closing the racial wealth disparity gap through affordable homeownership programs. The organization operates with an activist mission to challenge predatory lending and corporate real estate practices while serving its membership base through direct services.

NACA offers character-based mortgage lending that disregards credit scores, eliminating traditional barriers to homeownership. Their signature mortgage product features no down payment, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and below-market fixed interest rates (5.625% for 30-year, 5.125% for 20-year, and 5% for 15-year mortgages as of April 2026). The organization provides HUD-approved housing counseling—representing 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally—and has facilitated 75,000 mortgages with $20 billion in mortgage commitments. They have helped 500,000 homeowners achieve homeownership through their programs.

NACA distinguishes itself through its advocacy-driven model and character-based underwriting philosophy that prioritizes member circumstances over credit history. The organization actively fights predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors, operates volunteer-driven programming (NACtivist network), and hosts nationwide multi-day "Achieve the Dream" events in cities including Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers, St. Louis, and Hartford. Their membership base participates in the organization's mission through both service utilization and activist engagement, creating an integrated model of lending and advocacy.

While NACA's offerings are genuinely compelling for eligible members, the organization requires active participation in their programs and events to qualify for mortgages. The website emphasizes "character-based" lending but does not detail specific income requirements, debt-to-income ratios, or other underwriting criteria. Their services appear geographically centered on specific metropolitan areas with scheduled events, potentially limiting accessibility for rural or underserved populations outside event locations. The organization operates primarily as a membership-based model rather than a traditional lender, which may require more time commitment than conventional mortgage processes.
pros: No credit score consideration—character-based lending focuses on circumstances rather than credit history
No down payment required on mortgages, eliminating the largest barrier to homeownership for many buyers
No closing costs or fees, reducing total cost of homeownership by thousands of dollars
Below-market fixed interest rates (5% to 5.625% as of April 2026) without adjustable-rate risk
No mortgage insurance requirement, further reducing monthly payment obligations
Provides 30% of all HUD-approved housing counseling nationally, indicating deep expertise and scale
Active advocacy against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors protects community interests
cons: Membership model requires active participation and attendance at multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' events to qualify, creating time barriers
Limited geographic accessibility with events scheduled only in specific cities (Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers, St. Louis, Hartford)
Website provides minimal detail on actual underwriting criteria, income requirements, or debt limits despite 'character-based' claims
No clear timeline specified for mortgage process from initial consultation to closing
Appears to be nonprofit model serving activist members rather than general consumer market, potentially limiting accessibility
best_for: First-time homebuyers in underserved communities with limited savings for down payments or closing costs
Borrowers with poor or limited credit history who would be rejected by traditional mortgage lenders
Community activists and individuals committed to advocacy against predatory lending and corporate real estate practices
Low-to-moderate income families seeking mortgages with below-market rates and no mortgage insurance
diagnosis: NACA is best for first-time homebuyers with limited credit history and minimal savings who can commit time to the membership-based qualification process through in-person events. The critical caveat is that access requires geographic proximity to scheduled 'Achieve the Dream' events and active participation—this is not a traditional lender for those seeking immediate, remote mortgage processing.
services: Character-based mortgage lending without credit score consideration
No down payment mortgages for qualified members
Fixed-rate mortgages (15, 20, and 30-year terms) with below-market interest rates
HUD-approved housing counseling and financial education
Member portal for file management, document uploads, and status tracking
Multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' events in major metropolitan areas for qualification and member engagement
Volunteer opportunities through NACtivist program for community activism
Lender partnership network connecting members with vetted real estate agents, home inspectors, and settlement agents
Predatory landlord advocacy and tenant complaint documentation services
Affordable modular home programs in select markets
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 810 Dutch Square Blvd Suite 220, Columbia, SC 29210
